evals.prompt: I just finished implementing user authentication with JWT tokens. There are changes in auth/service.go, auth/service_test.go, and I also fixed a typo in README.md while I was at it. Can you commit everything properly?
evals.expected_output: Separates the auth feature (service + test) into one feat commit and the README typo fix into a separate docs commit
expectations: Creates at least 2 separate commits (auth changes and docs change)
Auth commit uses feat type and groups service.go with service_test.go
README commit uses docs type
Each commit message follows type: description format without scope
Git hooks validate each commit message

evals.prompt: I have a big service.go file where I refactored the error handling (renamed variables, extracted a helper) and also added a new endpoint for user deletion. These are mixed in the same file. Please commit them.
evals.expected_output: Commits the file under the dominant change type since whole-file staging cannot split within a file, or asks the user which concern is primary
expectations: Recognizes mixed concerns in a single file
Does not attempt hunk-level staging
Stages the whole file with git add
Commits under the dominant change type (feat or refactor)
May ask the user which concern is primary when ambiguous

evals.expected_output: Autonomously analyzes the working tree, determines the commit strategy based on semantic diff analysis, and creates appropriate commits
expectations: Runs git status, git diff HEAD, git diff --cached, and git log
Performs semantic diff analysis before committing
Git hooks validate messages transparently
Reports final result with commits created and status

evals.expected_output: Detects clean working directory and reports it without attempting any commits
evals.setup_note: Run this eval on a clean repo with no uncommitted changes
expectations: Runs git status and git diff HEAD
Detects no changes to commit
Reports clean working directory
Does not attempt to create any commits
Does not ask unnecessary questions

evals.prompt: I forgot to add the config file to my last commit. Can you amend it to include config/settings.yaml?
evals.expected_output: Stages config/settings.yaml and amends the last commit, reusing or updating the message
expectations: Stages config/settings.yaml with git add
Uses git commit --amend
Git hooks validate the message during amend
Does not create a new separate commit
Reports the amended commit

evals.prompt: commit the changes in the binary image file logo.png and the text change in README.md
evals.expected_output: Handles binary file gracefully, commits both changes with appropriate type
expectations: Recognizes logo.png as binary
Stages both files with git add
Creates commit with validated message
Uses appropriate commit type (chore or docs depending on grouping)

evals.prompt: /commit unstage the payment changes and only commit the auth changes
evals.expected_output: Uses git reset HEAD to unstage payment files, then commits only auth changes
expectations: Uses git reset HEAD for payment files to unstage them
Only auth-related changes are in the final commit
Git hooks validate the commit message
Reports what was committed and what was left unstaged
